_comment: Telegram bot config. Allowlist is enforced FIRST — anyone not on it gets silent-dropped before Claude sees the message. Copy to config.json and fill in the placeholders.
bot_username: <paste your bot username without the @, e.g. my_dispatch_bot>
_display_name_comment: Shown in the /restart ack message and saved as the assistant role label in transcripts. Purely cosmetic.
_user_ids_comment: Numeric Telegram user_ids that can DM the bot directly. Easiest way to find yours: message @userinfobot.
_chat_ids_comment: Numeric group chat_ids the bot should respond in (when @-mentioned). Group IDs are negative. Send a message in the group, then check getUpdates or @userinfobot in the group.
_claude_command_comment: Path to the claude CLI. Use 'claude' if it's on PATH (in your launchd/systemd env); otherwise an absolute path like '/Users/me/.local/bin/claude'.
_working_dir_comment: Directory Claude Code runs in. Set this to a folder Claude should treat as 'home' — that's where it can read SKILL files, see project files, etc. Defaults to the bot's own dir.
_claude_args_comment: Args passed to `claude -p` after the prompt. --dangerously-skip-permissions is needed for -p mode because there's no interactive prompt for MCP tool approvals. For Opus with 1M context (Team plan only), set this to ['--model', 'opus', '--dangerously-skip-permissions'] AND set claude_env below.
_claude_env_comment: Env vars merged into the Claude subprocess. For Opus 4.7 + 1M context window: {'ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL': 'claude-opus-4-7[1m]'}. For max extended thinking: add 'CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL': 'max'.
_transcript_dir_comment: Where chat transcripts live. Defaults to ./transcripts next to config.json. On macOS, KEEP THIS OUT OF ~/Documents — that folder is TCC-protected and python3 will lose access on Command Line Tools updates.
_transcript_comment: Per-chat history files are capped at the last N turns to keep context tight.
_media_dir_comment: Where downloaded Telegram media (photos, documents) get stored. Defaults to ./media/incoming next to config.json. Same TCC warning as transcript_dir.
_response_comment: Telegram message hard limit is 4096; cap below that for breathing room. Longer Claude outputs split across multiple messages.
